Versa Networks/ Documentation/ FlexVNF/ CLI Guide

About the CLI Guide

The Versa FlexVNF CLI Guide documents how to use the command-line interface (CLI) to configure and monitor Versa Networks FlexVNF appliances running VOS (Versa Operating System).

This guide covers:

  • Using configuration statements to configure network devices and services
  • Managing device configurations (commit, rollback, compare)
  • Using operational and show commands to monitor devices
  • Complete syntax reference for all 2076 configuration, operational, and service commands
Software VersionVOS 22.1.4 and later
PlatformVersa FlexVNF (physical and virtual appliances)
CLI FrameworkConfD (Tail-f) management framework
Total Commands2076 commands, 16516 parameters
Access MethodSSH → bash shell → cli command
Note: This reference was auto-generated by parsing every ? help output from a live Versa FlexVNF running VOS 22.1.4. Every parameter, keyword, and sub-command found on the device is documented.

CLI Overview

The Versa FlexVNF CLI is a command shell built on the ConfD (Tail-f) management framework. It provides a Junos-style hierarchical command structure for configuring all aspects of the FlexVNF platform including routing, SD-WAN, security, NAT, QoS, and more.

The CLI supports two primary modes of operation:

  • Operational mode — For monitoring, diagnostics, and system operations
  • Configuration mode — For modifying device configuration

Accessing the CLI

$ ssh admin@<device-ip> admin@hostname:~$ cli admin@hostname-cli>
Note: On Director-managed devices, local commit is blocked by default. Run vsh allow-cli from bash first, answer yes, then enter password.

CLI Modes

Operational Mode

Default mode after login. Used for monitoring, diagnostics, and system operations without modifying configuration.

Prompt

admin@hostname-cli>

Available Commands

show
Display system information, configuration, and operational state
request
System-level requests (reboot, restart, clear, diagnostics)
ping
Ping an IPv4/IPv6 host
traceroute
Trace route to a host
mtr
Combined ping + traceroute
tcpdump
Packet capture on interfaces
monitor
Real-time debugging and log monitoring
file
File operations (copy, delete, list, show, compare, rename)
configure
Enter configuration mode
ssh / scp / wget
Remote connectivity and file transfer

Configuration Mode

Allows modification of device configuration. Enter with configure from operational mode.

Prompt

admin@hostname-cli(config)%

Available Commands

set
Set a configuration parameter
delete
Delete a configuration element
edit
Navigate into a sub-element for editing
show
Show configuration at current level
commit
Commit current set of changes
compare
Show configuration differences
validate
Validate configuration without committing
rollback
Roll back to last committed version
load / save
Load/save configuration from/to ASCII file
top / up / exit
Navigate configuration hierarchy

CLI Conventions

Output Modifiers (Pipe Commands)

| match <pattern>
Filter output lines containing pattern. Example: show configuration | match bgp
| nomore
Disable pagination. Example: show route | nomore
| display set
Show config as set commands. Example: show configuration | display set
| display xml
Show config in XML format
| count
Count matching lines
Note: Use | match (NOT | include). Use | nomore (NOT | no-more). These are ConfD-specific modifiers, different from Cisco IOS.

Configuration Display Paths

Configuration (set commands)
orgs org-services <org>
Operational (show commands)
orgs org <org>

Placeholder Conventions

<org-name>
Organization/tenant name (e.g., VERSA)
<vr-name>
Routing instance name (e.g., INET-1-Transport-VR)
<interface>
Interface name (e.g., vni-0/0.0)
<ip-address>
IPv4 or IPv6 address
<asn>
BGP Autonomous System Number
1 (in protocol paths)
Protocol instance ID (typically 1 on FlexVNF)

set protocols bgp 1

Syntax Description Options

Syntax

set protocols bgp 1

Description

BGP configuration

Options

aggregate
Prefix Aggregation parameters
as-origination-interval
Minimum amount of time that must elapse between successive advertisements of UPDATE messages that report changes within the advertising BGP speaker's own autonomous system
as-override
Replace Neighbor AS numbers in the AS-path with the Local AS Number
as-path-dotted
Process as-path in dotted notation(For example 1.1 for 65536) in all match policy configuration
bfd-liveness-detection
Bidirectional Forwarding Detection (BFD) options
cluster-id
Cluster identifier
community-4-byte
Process the community in 4-byte format
damping
Enable route-flap dampening
debug
Configure debug flags
description
Text description
disable-extended-message-length-capability
Disable BGP extended message length capability
ebgp-preference
Preference(admin distance) for eBGP routes
enable-alarms
Enables alarm generation
family
Protocol family for NLRIs in updates
fast-external-fallover
Bring down the EBGP sessions for directly connected neighbors if the connected interface becomes down.
graceful-restart
Graceful restart capability parameters
group
Define a peer group
hold-time
Hold time used when negotiating with a peer
ibgp-preference
Preference(admin distance) for iBGP routes
local-address
IP Address or the the name of interface the primary IP address of which should be used
local-as
Local autonomous system number
local-as-mode
Mode which decides the AS used for peering, AS numbers added in AS path while importing & exporting route when local-as is configured in group/neighbor
next-hop-unchanged
Keep original nexthop of the BGP routes unchanged while sending to all EBGP neighbors.
outbound-route-filter
Dynamically negotiated cooperative route filtering
passive
Do not send open messages to a peer
password
The MD5 password used by this BGP instance <4..128 chars long>
path-selection
Configure path selection strategy
peer-as
Remote Autonomous system number in plain number or 'higher 16bits'.'Lower 16 bits' (asdot notation) format
peer-debug
Configure neighbor debug flags
prefix-limit
Prefix-limit related parameters when importing routes from neighbor.
prefix-list
Create a prefix-list or enter prefix-list command mode
relax-first-as-check
Relax the check that the first(left-most) AS number in an AS path received from an EBGP neighbor is same as neighbor's AS
remove-private
Remove all private AS numbers from the received AS-path
route-flap
Configure route flap options for BGP
route-reflector-client
Configure all neighbors in this instance as route reflector clients
router-id
IPv4 address or name of the interface the primary IPv4 address of which should be used for BGP router-id
routing-peer-policy
Peer/Peer-group policy configuration
set-stale-local-pref-in-rib
Configure local preference for stale routes installed in RIB
shutdown
Disable all static neighbors in the BGP instance
sla-community
It supports two formats.
sla-profile
BGP SLA profile configuration
soft-reconfiguration
Keep received routes even if rejected by routing-peer-policy.
soo
Site of Origin Community to use while redistributing routes.
stale-routes-gr-behavior
If enabled, stale routes shall be immune to route-deletes (default: disabled)
suppress-peer-as
Do not advertise routes received from an EBGP neighbor to another neighbor in same AS as originating neighbor
ttl
TTL value for the session (1..255)

Command Mode

Configuration mode

Release Information

Command supported from VOS 22.1.4 and later releases.

set protocols bgp 1 aggregate

Syntax Description Options

Syntax

set protocols bgp 1 aggregate (family)

Description

Prefix Aggregation parameters

Options

family
Protocol family for Aggregate routes

Command Mode

Configuration mode

Release Information

Command supported from VOS 22.1.4 and later releases.

set protocols bgp 1 graceful-restart

Syntax Description Options

Syntax

set protocols bgp 1 graceful-restart

Description

Graceful restart capability parameters

Options

dynamic-peer-restart-time
Minimum time to wait for dynamic peers to reconnect after BGP process restarts
enable
Enable graceful restart
family
Protocol family for enabling graceful restart parameters
helper
Graceful restart helper enable/disable.
maximum-restart-time
Set the max time needed to restart and come back up
multiplier
Graceful Restart time multiplier (1..255)
preserve-routes-across-restarts
Preserve routes across restarts;; Default : disable
recovery-time
Set the estimated recovery time after a restart occurs
select-defer-time
The maximum time (in seconds) following local restart that BGP process will wait before performing route selection
stalepath-time
Maximum time BGP waits before removing stale routes from a neighbor after neighbor session restarts

Command Mode

Configuration mode

Release Information

Command supported from VOS 22.1.4 and later releases.

set protocols bgp 1 path-selection

Syntax Description Options

Syntax

set protocols bgp 1 path-selection (always-compare-med | as-path-ignore | as-path-multipath-relax | cisco-non-deterministic)

Description

Configure path selection strategy

Options

always-compare-med
Always compare MED values, regardless of neighbor AS
as-path-ignore
Exclude AS-path from BGP best path computation
as-path-multipath-relax
Allow differing AS paths to be considered for multipath if their lengths are equal.
cisco-non-deterministic
Use Cisco IOS nondeterministic path selection algorithm

Configuration mode

Command supported from VOS 22.1.4 and later releases.

Command Mode

Configuration mode

Release Information

Command supported from VOS 22.1.4 and later releases.

set protocols bgp 1 route-flap

Syntax Description Options

Syntax

set protocols bgp 1 route-flap (free-max-time | reuse-array-size | reuse-max-time | reuse-size)

Description

Configure route flap options for BGP

Options

free-max-time
The maximum time for which a penalty shall be remembered
reuse-array-size
This is the size of the reuse index arrays
reuse-max-time
The time corresponding to the last reuse list
reuse-size
This is the number of reuse lists.

Command Mode

Configuration mode

Release Information

Command supported from VOS 22.1.4 and later releases.

set protocols bgp 1 route-reflector-client

Syntax Description Options

Syntax

set protocols bgp 1 route-reflector-client

Description

BGP configuration

Options

aggregate
Prefix Aggregation parameters
as-origination-interval
Minimum amount of time that must elapse between successive advertisements of UPDATE messages that report changes within the advertising BGP speaker's own autonomous system
as-override
Replace Neighbor AS numbers in the AS-path with the Local AS Number
as-path-dotted
Process as-path in dotted notation(For example 1.1 for 65536) in all match policy configuration
bfd-liveness-detection
Bidirectional Forwarding Detection (BFD) options
cluster-id
Cluster identifier
community-4-byte
Process the community in 4-byte format
damping
Enable route-flap dampening
debug
Configure debug flags
description
Text description
disable-extended-message-length-capability
Disable BGP extended message length capability
ebgp-preference
Preference(admin distance) for eBGP routes
enable-alarms
Enables alarm generation
family
Protocol family for NLRIs in updates
fast-external-fallover
Bring down the EBGP sessions for directly connected neighbors if the connected interface becomes down.
graceful-restart
Graceful restart capability parameters
group
Define a peer group
hold-time
Hold time used when negotiating with a peer
ibgp-preference
Preference(admin distance) for iBGP routes
local-address
IP Address or the the name of interface the primary IP address of which should be used
local-as-mode
Mode which decides the AS used for peering, AS numbers added in AS path while importing & exporting route when local-as is configured in group/neighbor
next-hop-unchanged
Keep original nexthop of the BGP routes unchanged while sending to all EBGP neighbors.
outbound-route-filter
Dynamically negotiated cooperative route filtering
passive
Do not send open messages to a peer
password
The MD5 password used by this BGP instance <4..128 chars long>
path-selection
Configure path selection strategy
peer-as
Remote Autonomous system number in plain number or 'higher 16bits'.'Lower 16 bits' (asdot notation) format
peer-debug
Configure neighbor debug flags
prefix-limit
Prefix-limit related parameters when importing routes from neighbor.
prefix-list
Create a prefix-list or enter prefix-list command mode
relax-first-as-check
Relax the check that the first(left-most) AS number in an AS path received from an EBGP neighbor is same as neighbor's AS
remove-private
Remove all private AS numbers from the received AS-path
route-flap
Configure route flap options for BGP
router-id
IPv4 address or name of the interface the primary IPv4 address of which should be used for BGP router-id
routing-peer-policy
Peer/Peer-group policy configuration
set-stale-local-pref-in-rib
Configure local preference for stale routes installed in RIB
shutdown
Disable all static neighbors in the BGP instance
sla-community
It supports two formats.
sla-profile
BGP SLA profile configuration
soft-reconfiguration
Keep received routes even if rejected by routing-peer-policy.
soo
Site of Origin Community to use while redistributing routes.
stale-routes-gr-behavior
If enabled, stale routes shall be immune to route-deletes (default: disabled)
suppress-peer-as
Do not advertise routes received from an EBGP neighbor to another neighbor in same AS as originating neighbor
ttl
TTL value for the session (1..255)

Configuration mode

Command Mode

Configuration mode

Release Information

Command supported from VOS 22.1.4 and later releases.

set protocols bgp 1 sla-community

Syntax Description Options Accepted Values

Syntax

set protocols bgp 1 sla-community

Description

It supports two formats. The first format supports extended community as 'sla:IPv4 address in dotted notation followed by 2 byte decimal with maximum value of 65535' or 'sla:4 byte decimal with a value between 1 and 4294967295 followed by letter L:2 byte decimal with a maximum value of 65535' or 'sla:2 byte decimal with a value between 1 and 65535:4 byte decimal with a maximum value o

Options

<string>

Accepted Values

<string>

vni-0/1

vni-0/4

Command Mode

Configuration mode

Release Information

Command supported from VOS 22.1.4 and later releases.

set interfaces vni-0/0

Syntax Description Options

Syntax

set interfaces vni-0/0

Description

Versa Physical interface

Options

bandwidth
Interface bandwidth in kbps
description
Description for the interface
dhcp-trusted
DHCP trusted port.
dynamic-interface
Dynamic port profile will be applied
enable
Enable/disable this interface
ether-options
Specify Ethernet options
ethernet-segment
EVPN multihoming configuration for Ethernet segment
hold-time
Hold the link state in down state for configured interval before declaring it as operational when transitioning from down to up state.
mirror-interface
Used for traffic mirroring
mtu
MTU of the interface
native-vlan-id
Specifies the Native VLAN ID for the interface
outer-tpid
Outer VLAN tag protocol id supported on the port
poe
Power over Ethernet
pppoe-base-interface
Used as PPPoE base interface
promiscuous
Enable or disable promiscuous mode on this interface
sfp-options
Specify SFP options
standby-options
Standby configuration
unit
Configure logical interfaces under this interface
virtual-wire
Member of virtual wire pair

Command Mode

Configuration mode

Release Information

Command supported from VOS 22.1.4 and later releases.

set interfaces vni-0/0 unit

Syntax Description

Syntax

set interfaces vni-0/0 unit

Description

Configure or display settings for this command path.

Command Mode

Configuration mode

Release Information

Command supported from VOS 22.1.4 and later releases.

set interfaces vni-0/0 unit 0

Syntax Description Options

Syntax

set interfaces vni-0/0 unit 0

Description

Configure logical interfaces under this interface

Options

bandwidth
Interface bandwidth in kbps
description
Description for the Unit interface
enable
Enable/disable this sub-interface
family
Specify Bridge or IPv4 or IPv6 attributes
inner-vlan-id
Inner VLAN ID for dual-tagged sub-interface
mode
Interface mode
mtu
MTU of the sub-interface
publish-address
scalable-group-tag
Scalable group tag configuration
standby-options
Standby configuration
vlan-id
VLAN identifier for single tagged interface, or outer VLAN identifier for dual tagged interface

Command Mode

Configuration mode

Release Information

Command supported from VOS 22.1.4 and later releases.

set interfaces vni-0/0 unit 0 family

Syntax Description Options

Syntax

set interfaces vni-0/0 unit 0 family (bridge)

Description

Specify Bridge or IPv4 or IPv6 attributes

Options

bridge

Command Mode

Configuration mode

Release Information

Command supported from VOS 22.1.4 and later releases.

set interfaces vni-0/0 unit 0 family inet

Syntax Description Options

Syntax

set interfaces vni-0/0 unit 0 family inet (address | dhcp | dhcp-client-options | directed-broadcast | fqdn | proxy-arp | sla-endpoint)

Description

Configure or display settings for this command path.

Options

address
IP Address/Mask length
dhcp
Use DHCP to obtain address
dhcp-client-options
Configure DHCP client options
directed-broadcast
Enable/disable directed broadcast
fqdn
Fully qualified domain name
proxy-arp
Configure proxy ARP
sla-endpoint
All IPv4 addresses assigned to this interface will be advertised as sla-endpoints.

Command Mode

Configuration mode

Release Information

Command supported from VOS 22.1.4 and later releases.

set interfaces vni-0/0 unit 0 family inet6

Syntax Description Options

Syntax

set interfaces vni-0/0 unit 0 family inet6

Description

Configure or display settings for this command path.

Options

address
Configure IPv6 Address
delegated-prefix-pool
Configure IPv6 Address using delegated prefix pool
dhcp
Use DHCP to obtain address
fqdn
Fully qualified domain name
hop-limit
Configure hop-limit for outgoing IPv6 packets
interface-identifier
Interface identifier (::x:x:x:x) to generate IPv6 address
mode
Configure IPv6 Interface host/router mode
neighbor-discovery
Configure IPv6 Neighbor discovery related parameters
proxy-ndp
Configure Neighbor Discovery (NDP) proxy

Command Mode

Configuration mode

Release Information

Command supported from VOS 22.1.4 and later releases.

set interfaces ae0

Syntax Description Options

Syntax

set interfaces ae0

Description

Versa Aggregated Ethernet

Options

aggregated-ethernet-options
Specify options for this Aggregated Ethernet link
bandwidth
Interface bandwidth in kbps
description
Description for the interface
dhcp-trusted
DHCP trusted port.
enable
Enable/disable this interface
ethernet-segment
EVPN multihoming configuration for Ethernet segment
mirror-interface
Used for traffic mirroring
mtu
MTU of the interface
native-vlan-id
Specifies the Native VLAN ID for the interface
pppoe-base-interface
Used as PPPoE base interface
promiscuous
Enable or disable promiscuous mode on this interface
standby-options
Standby configuration
unit
Configure logical interfaces under this interface
virtual-wire
Member of virtual wire pair

Command Mode

Configuration mode

Release Information

Command supported from VOS 22.1.4 and later releases.

set interfaces tvi-0/22

Syntax Description Options

Syntax

set interfaces tvi-0/22

Description

Versa Tunnel Virtual interface

Options

description
Description for the interface
enable
Enable/disable this interface
ethernet-segment
EVPN multihoming configuration for Ethernet segment
mirror-interface
Used for traffic mirroring
mode
Interface mode
mtu
MTU of the interface
nsh-capable
Enable NSH header encapsulation
service-function-nsh
Act as a IETF sfc service function with no service function forwarder configuration
transport-type
Choice of transport encapsulation
type
Tunnel type
unit
Configure default logical interface under this interface

Command Mode

Configuration mode

Release Information

Command supported from VOS 22.1.4 and later releases.

set interfaces lo-0/0

Syntax Description Options

Syntax

set interfaces lo-0/0

Description

Configure or display settings for this command path.

Options

Command Mode

Configuration mode

Release Information

Command supported from VOS 22.1.4 and later releases.

set interfaces tvi-0/22 unit 0

Syntax Description Options

Syntax

set interfaces tvi-0/22 unit 0 (bandwidth | description | enable | family)

Description

Configure default logical interface under this interface

Options

bandwidth
Interface bandwidth in kbps
description
Description for the Unit interface
enable
Enable/disable this sub-interface
family
Specify Bridge or IPv4 or IPv6 attributes

Command Mode

Configuration mode

Release Information

Command supported from VOS 22.1.4 and later releases.

set interfaces eth-0/0

Syntax Description Options

Syntax

set interfaces eth-0/0

Description

Management interfaces

Options

description
Interface description
disabled
Administrative state
duplex
Duplex mode
enabled
Administrative state
mtu
MTU (Max transmission unit) size
promiscuous
Enable or disable promiscuous mode
speed
Speed of the interface (Mb/s)
type
Management-internal/Management-external
unit
Logical interface configuration

set system set system identification set system vnf-manager set system session set system services set system users set system ssh set system sd-wan set system sd-wan site set system sd-wan transport-domains set system sd-wan controllers set system dns set system login set system subjugate set system time-zone set system arp set system arp age-interval set system arp gratuitous-arp set system arp rate-limit set system banner-text set system bw-measurement set system bw-measurement client-options set system bw-measurement client-options background-traffic-threshold set system bw-measurement client-options monitored-bandwidth-usage set system bw-measurement client-options test-limit set system bw-measurement common-options set system bw-measurement monitor set system bw-measurement server set system bw-measurement server routing-instance set system bw-measurement server routing-instances set system console set system console enable set system console idle-timeout set system dataplane-latency-measurement set system dhcp-parameters set system external-aaa set system log-compress set system maintenance-mode set system password-policy set system platform set system service-options set system service-scaling set system syslog set system traffic-director set system upgrade-options set system user-lockout set system wwan set system sd-wan site wan-interfaces

set system

Syntax Description Options

Syntax

set system

Description

System configuration and statistics

Options

arp
Session timeout configuration
banner-text
CLI/SSH/MOTD banners
bw-measurement
system level bandwidth measurement commands
confd-parameters
console
Console connection config parameters
core-profile
dataplane-latency-measurement
System Level Dataplane Latency Configuration
device
dhcp-parameters
System level DHCP parameters config
dns
Domain name system
external-aaa
External Authentication/Authorization/Accounting
identification
System Identification
log-compress
Log compression/rotation parameters
login
Configure login monitoring parameters
maintenance-mode
Maintenance mode
parameters
password-policy
Configure login monitoring parameters
platform
Platform specific configurations
sd-wan
Global parameters for SDWAN appliance
security
service-options
Service options configuration (vsboot parameters)
service-scaling
Service scaling configuration
services
Enable/Disable system/Linux services via datapath
session
Session timeout configuration
smart-aggregator
ssh
Configure SSH parameters
subjugate
Subjugation parameters (master Versa-Director IP)
syslog
Syslog Server
time-zone
Set system time-zone
traffic-director
configure traffic-director related
upgrade-options
Upgrade options
user-lockout
Configure user lockout(faillock)
users
Add users to the system
vnf-manager
VNF Manager configuration for appliance
wwan
Wireless WAN config

Command Mode

Configuration mode

Release Information

Command supported from VOS 22.1.4 and later releases.

set system identification

Syntax Description Options

Syntax

set system identification

Description

System Identification

Options

contact
Contact information of the person responsible for this node
description
Textual description of the entity
id
Unique ID of this node
latitude
Latitude of the node's location
location
Physical location of this node
longitude
Longitude of the node's location
name
An administratively-assigned name for this node
objid
Object ID (SNMPv2-MIB::system::sysObjectID)
services
A value which indicates the set of services that this entity may potentially offer

Command Mode

Configuration mode

Release Information

Command supported from VOS 22.1.4 and later releases.

set system vnf-manager

Syntax Description Options

Syntax

set system vnf-manager (ip-addresses | vnf-mgmt-interfaces)

Description

VNF Manager configuration for appliance

Options

ip-addresses
IPv4 addresses of VNF manager
vnf-mgmt-interfaces
Management interface: tvi, gre, gre6, eogre, ipsec, pppoe, vni, tdlo, t1e1 or ae

Command Mode

Configuration mode

Release Information

Command supported from VOS 22.1.4 and later releases.

set system session

Syntax Description Options

Syntax

set system session

Description

Session timeout configuration

Options

additional-flow-key-attribute
Additional attribute used to identify a flow
allow-unsupported-protocol
Option to control IP Protocol validity check
check-tcp-syn
Allow the session creation only on existence of the TCP SYN flag
interim-update
Interim update parameters
limit-icmp-errors
Limit the rate of ICMP errors generated by VOS (per second) per worker thread
log-unknown-hostbound-packets
Log packets unknown packets destined to VOS IP address
reevaluate-reverse-flow
Re-evaluate the packet policy for first packet of a reverse flow if the forward flow has been marked as drop
send-icmp-unreachable
Send ICMP unreachable to source during route lookup failures
session-purge-batch-size
Number of sessions to inspect in each cycle of session aging process
session-reevaluate
Session reevaluate during config/route change
tcp-adjust-mss
Enable TCP MSS adjustment
tcp-secure-reset
Follow the secure criteria for accepting RST packets
tcp-send-reset
Send RST to the source if the first packet of a new TCP flow does not have SYN flag set
timeout-default
Default timeout for sessions in seconds
timeout-hard
Hard session timeout in seconds
timeout-icmp
Idle timeout for ICMP sessions in seconds
timeout-tcp
Idle timeout for TCP sessions in seconds
timeout-tcp-halfclosed
Timeout for TCP sessions after receiving 1st FIN and before 2nd FIN or RST, in seconds
timeout-tcp-halfopen
Timeout for TCP sessions after receiving SYN and before completion of 3-way handshake, in seconds
timeout-tcpwait
TCP wait timeout (after receiving 2 FIN or RST) in seconds
timeout-udp
Idle timeout for UDP sessions in seconds

Command Mode

Configuration mode

Release Information

Command supported from VOS 22.1.4 and later releases.

set system services

Syntax Description Options

Syntax

set system services (access-list | management | sftp | ssh | www)

Description

Enable/Disable system/Linux services via datapath

Options

access-list
ACLs for SSH/SNMP
management
Enable disable services on management interface (eth0)
sftp
Allow/Disallow SFTP (SSH has to be enabled)
ssh
Allow/Disallow SSH access
www
Allow/Disallow www access

Command Mode

Configuration mode

Release Information

Command supported from VOS 22.1.4 and later releases.

set system ssh

Syntax Description Options

Syntax

set system ssh (allow-ssh-through-ptvi | client-alive-interval | compression | host-key-algorithms | login-grace-time | max-concurrent-logins)

Description

Configure SSH parameters

Options

allow-ssh-through-ptvi
Allow ssh access through paired tvi
client-alive-interval
Client Alive Interval value
compression
Enable/Disable compression
host-key-algorithms
Set SSH host key algorithms
login-grace-time
Disconnect after this time if user not successfully logged in
max-concurrent-logins
Maximum number of concurrent logins on this VOS

sla-monitor

transport-domains

Command Mode

Configuration mode

Release Information

Command supported from VOS 22.1.4 and later releases.

set system sd-wan site

Syntax Description Options

Syntax

set system sd-wan site (chassis-id | hot-standby | paired-site | provider-org | region-id-list | site-id | site-type | wan-interfaces)

Description

SDWAN site configuration

Options

chassis-id
Unique identifier for chassis
hot-standby
Enable/Disable hot standby mode, disabled by default
paired-site
Configuration for active-active paired branch
provider-org
SDWAN Appliance owner org
region-id-list
List of region(s) to which the site belongs
site-id
Unique site identifier number
site-type
SDWAN site type
wan-interfaces
WAN access interfaces

Command Mode

Release Information

set system login

Syntax Description Options

Syntax

set system login (ban | max-retries | white-list)

Description

Configure login monitoring parameters

Options

ban
Enable/Disable monitoring login attempts
max-retries
Number of failed login attempts after which host will be banned
white-list
Specify IP addresses that should be exempt from being banned (ignore any failed login attempts from these addresses)

disabled

enabled

Command Mode

set system arp

Syntax Description Options

Syntax

set system arp (age-interval | gratuitous-arp | rate-limit)

age-interval

gratuitous-arp

rate-limit

Command Mode

Configuration mode

Release Information

Command supported from VOS 22.1.4 and later releases.

set protocols ospf 1

Syntax Description Options

Syntax

set protocols ospf 1 (area | default-information | description | enable-alarms | ext-preference | int-preference | reference-bandwidth | router-id)

Description

OSPF instance configuration

Options

area
Configure an OSPF area
default-information
Configuration to announce default route
description
Text description
enable-alarms
Enables alarm generation
ext-preference
Preference(admin distance) for external routes
int-preference
Preference(admin distance) for internal routes
reference-bandwidth
Bandwidth for calculating metric defaults
router-id
The router-id for this OSPF instance

Command Mode

Configuration mode

Release Information

Command supported from VOS 22.1.4 and later releases.

set protocols ospf 1 area

Syntax Description Options

Syntax

set protocols ospf 1 area (Area)

Description

Configure or display settings for this command path.

Options

Area

Command Mode

Configuration mode

Release Information

Command supported from VOS 22.1.4 and later releases.

set protocols ospf 1 default-information

Syntax Description Options

Syntax

set protocols ospf 1 default-information (always | metric | originate | prefix-list | tag | type)

Description

Configuration to announce default route

Options

always
Announce default route to OSPF neighbors based on the default-information configuration of the presence of default route in the routing table
metric
Metric value for the default route to be announced to OSPF neighbors
originate
Announce default route to OSPF neighbors
prefix-list
Default route will be announced to OSPF neighbors based on the default-information configuration only if there are routes in the routing-table matching this prefix-list
tag
Tag value for the default route to be announced to OSPF neighbors
type
External type of the default route to be announced to OSPF neighbors

Command Mode

Configuration mode

Release Information

Command supported from VOS 22.1.4 and later releases.

set protocols ospf 1 default-information always

Syntax Description Options

Syntax

set protocols ospf 1 default-information always

Description

Announce default route to OSPF neighbors based on the default-information configuration of the presence of default route in the routing table

Options

<Default>
false

Command Mode

set protocols ospf3 1

Syntax Description Options

Syntax

set protocols ospf3 1 (area | default-information | description | enable-alarms | ext-preference | int-preference | router-id | vpn-pe-ce-instance)

Description

OSPFv3 instance configuration

Options

area
Configure an OSPFv3 area
default-information
Configuration to announce default route
description
Text description
enable-alarms
Enables alarm generation
ext-preference
Preference(admin distance) for external routes
int-preference
Preference(admin distance) for internal routes
router-id
The router-id for this OSPF instance
vpn-pe-ce-instance
To specify router as provider-edge(PE) while using OSPF between provider edge (PE) routers and customer edge (CE) routers in a BGP/MPLS IP VPN.

Configuration mode

Release Information

Command supported from VOS 22.1.4 and later releases.

always

metric
Metric value for the default route to be announced to OSPF neighbors
originate
Announce default route to OSPF neighbors
prefix-list
Default route will be announced to OSPF neighbors based on the default-information configuration only if there are routes in the routing-table matching this prefix-list
tag

type

Command Mode

Configuration mode

Release Information

Command supported from VOS 22.1.4 and later releases.

set protocols rip 1

Syntax Description Options

Syntax

set protocols rip 1

Description

RIP instance configuration

Options

authentication-key
Authentication plain text password (key of length 1 to 16)
authentication-type
Authentication type
bfd-liveness-detection
Bidirectional Forwarding Detection (BFD) options
group
Define a group
holddown
Time interval to delete a route from routing table after its expiry (1-255 seconds)
md5-authentication-key
MD5 Authentication password (key of length 4 to 16)
preference
Preference(admin distance) for RIP routes
receive
Configure RIP receive options;;Default version-2
route-timeout
Delay before routes time out (1-255 seconds)
send
Configure RIP send options ;;Default multicast
update-interval
Interval between regular route updates (1-2147483 seconds)

Syntax Description

Syntax

set protocols pim

Description

Configure or display settings for this command path.

Syntax

Configure or display settings for this command path.

Command Mode

Configuration mode

Release Information

Command supported from VOS 22.1.4 and later releases.

set protocols pim 1

Syntax Description Options

Syntax

set protocols pim 1

Description

PIM configuration

Options

assert-timeout
Set assert timeout in seconds(5..210);;Default 180
candidate-bsr
Configure a candidate BSR.
candidate-rp
Configure a candidate RP.
cluster-list
List of cluster-ids to which the pim instance belongs.
enable-alarms
Enables alarm generation
interface
PIM interface options
join-prune-timeout
Set join/prune timeout in seconds(210..420);;Default 210
override-interval
Override interval value in milliseconds(500..6000 milliseconds);;Default 2500
pim-mrpf
RIB for PIM RPF lookup;;Default unicast
propagation-delay
Propagation delay value in milliseconds(250..2000 milliseconds);;Default 500
register-message-filter
Register filter options
rp
Rendezvous point properties
spt-threshold
Set shortest-path-tree threshold
ssm-groups
Group address range of SSM

Command Mode

Configuration mode

Release Information

Command supported from VOS 22.1.4 and later releases.

set protocols pim 1 pim-mrpf rp

Syntax Description Options

Syntax

set protocols pim 1 pim-mrpf rp (anycast-rp | static)

Description

Rendezvous point properties

Options

anycast-rp
Configure anycast RP address and associatedroutable address of other RPs as well as local address.This should be configured in all RPs serving the anycast RP address
static
Configure static PIM RPs

Command Mode

Configuration mode

Release Information

Command supported from VOS 22.1.4 and later releases.

set protocols pim 1 pim-mrpf spt-threshold

Syntax Description Options

Syntax

set protocols pim 1 pim-mrpf spt-threshold (infinity | rate-in-kbps | rate-in-pps)

Description

Set shortest-path-tree threshold

Options

infinity
No switchover to SPT
rate-in-kbps
Switchover to SPT after specified rate in kbps
rate-in-pps
Switchover to SPT after specified rate in pps;;Default 1

Configuration mode

Command Mode

Configuration mode

Release Information

Command supported from VOS 22.1.4 and later releases.

set protocols igmp 1

Syntax Description Options

Syntax

set protocols igmp 1 (interface | query-interval | query-last-member-interval | query-response-interval | robust-count)

Description

IGMP configuration

Options

interface
Interface options for IGMP
query-interval
Configure genral purpose query interval (2..31744 seconds);;Default 125
query-last-member-interval
Configure group specific query interval (1..25 seconds);;Default 1
query-response-interval
The query response interval (1..25 seconds);;Default 10
robust-count
Expected packet loss on a subnet(2..7);;Default 2

Command Mode

Configuration mode

Release Information

Command supported from VOS 22.1.4 and later releases.

set protocols igmp 1 interface

Syntax Description Options

Syntax

set protocols igmp 1 interface (Interface)

Description

Interface name

Options

<Ethernet unit Name (enet-X/X.[0-4095])>
<ae subinterface name (aeX.[0-4095])>
<lo subinterface name (loX.0)>
<lt subinterface name (lt-[0-15]/[0-199999].[0-4095])>
<tvi subinterface name (tvi-X/X.[0-4095])>
<gre subinterface name (gre-X/X.[0-4095])>
<gre6 subinterface name (gre6-X/X.[0-4095])>
<eogre subinterface name (eogre-X/X.[0-4095])>
<ipsec subinterface name (ipsec-X/X.[0-4095])>
<pppoe subinterface name (pppoe-X/X.[0-4095])>
<dsl subinterface name (dsl-X/X.[0-4095])>
<geneve subinterface name (geneve-X/X.[0-4095])>
<lt subinterface name (lt-X/X.[0-4095])>
<vni or t1e1 subinterface name (vni-X/X.[0-4095]) or t1e1-X/X.[0-4095]>

set routing-options

Syntax Description Options

Syntax

set routing-options

Description

Protocol-independent routing option config

Options

create-dynamic-gre-tunnels
Create dynamic GRE tunnels for L3 VPN endpoint PEs
evpn-alarms
Enables EVPN alarms
evpn-local-router-address
Local router address used as BGP nexthop for EVPN routes
evpn-local-router-interface
Local router interface address used as BGP nexthop for EVPN routes
intelligent-traffic-director
Configure intelligent-traffic-director functionality
mpls-vpn-local-router-address
Local router address used as BGP nexthop for VPN routes
mpls-vpn-local-router-interface
Local router interface address used as BGP nexthop for VPN routes
rib
rib specific configuration
static
Static routes

Command Mode

Configuration mode

Release Information

Command supported from VOS 22.1.4 and later releases.

set policy-options

Syntax Description Options

Syntax

set policy-options

Description

Routing policy option configuration

Options

instance-import
Define redistribution between routing-instances for an AFI and Unicast SAFI
instance-import-afi-safi
Define redistribution between routing-instances for an AFI and SAFI
prefix-list
Create a prefix-list or enter prefix-list command mode
redistribute-to
Enable route redistibution
redistribute-to-bgp
Name of policy used for redistribution to BGP
redistribute-to-bgp6
Name of policy used for redistribution to BGP4+
redistribute-to-ospf
Name of policy used for redistribution to OSPF
redistribute-to-ospf3
Name of policy used for redistribution to OSPFv3
redistribute-to-rip
Name of policy used for redistribution to RIP
redistribution-policy
Redistribution policy configuration

Command Mode

Configuration mode

Release Information

Command supported from VOS 22.1.4 and later releases.

set routing-options create-dynamic-gre-tunnels

Syntax Description Options

Syntax

set routing-options create-dynamic-gre-tunnels (evpn-alarms | evpn-local-router-address | evpn-local-router-interface | intelligent-traffic-director | mpls-vpn-local-router-address | mpls-vpn-local-router-interface | rib | static)

Description

Protocol-independent routing option config

Options

evpn-alarms
Enables EVPN alarms
evpn-local-router-address
Local router address used as BGP nexthop for EVPN routes
evpn-local-router-interface
Local router interface address used as BGP nexthop for EVPN routes
intelligent-traffic-director
Configure intelligent-traffic-director functionality
mpls-vpn-local-router-address
Local router address used as BGP nexthop for VPN routes
mpls-vpn-local-router-interface
Local router interface address used as BGP nexthop for VPN routes
rib
rib specific configuration
static
Static routes

Command Mode

Configuration mode

Release Information

Command supported from VOS 22.1.4 and later releases.

set routing-options create-dynamic-gre-tunnels evpn-alarms

Syntax Description Options Accepted Values

Syntax

Description

Enables EVPN alarms

Options

disabled

Accepted Values

disabled,enabled

set routing-options rib (inet-multicast | inet-unicast | inet6-multicast | inet6-unicast)

Description

rib specific configuration

Options

inet-multicast
ipv4 unicast family
inet-unicast
ipv4 unicast family
inet6-multicast
ipv6 unicast family
inet6-unicast
ipv6 unicast family

Command Mode

Configuration mode

Release Information

Command supported from VOS 22.1.4 and later releases.

aggregate

static

Command Mode

set orgs org-services VERSA crypto pki settings certificate-expiry

Syntax Description Options

Syntax

set orgs org-services VERSA crypto pki settings certificate-expiry (alert-before | alert-daily | repeat-interval)

Description

Certificate expiry alarm related settings

Options

alert-before
Raises alarm before this number of days
alert-daily
Raises alarm every day only for last week before expiry and until the certificate is replaced
repeat-interval
Repeats the alarm at this interval (in days)

Release Information

Command supported from VOS 22.1.4 and later releases.

set orgs org-services VERSA pbf

Syntax Description Options

Syntax

set orgs org-services VERSA pbf (application-cache | application-dynamic-detection | policies | url-category-cache)

Description

Policy based forwarding configuration

Options

application-cache
Enable/disable application detection using cache at the beginning of flow
application-dynamic-detection
Enable/disable application detection dynamically in the middle of flow
policies
PBF policies configuration
url-category-cache
Enable/disable URL category detection using cache at the beginning of flow

set orgs org-services VERSA twamp-cntrl client

Syntax Description Options

Syntax

Description

Configuration of the TWAMP Control-Client logical entity.

Options

client-enable
Indicates whether the device is allowed to operate as a TWAMP Control-Client.
client-timeout
The timeout value for the TWAMP-Client to wait for reply from TWAMP-Server before closing connection.

reflector-light-enable

reflector-light-mode-state
The state of the mode of the TWAMP-Light Session-Reflector
test-session
This structure is a container of test session managed objects

set orgs org-services VERSA cgnat pools DIA-Pool-INET-1

Syntax Description Options

Syntax

set orgs org-services VERSA cgnat pools DIA-Pool-INET-1

Description

List of NAT pools

Options

address
Address and prefix (DEPRECATED, use address-list)
address-allocation
Address allocation scheme
address-list
List of addresses and/or prefixes
address-range
Source address range (DEPRECATED, use address-range-list)
address-range-list
Source address range
description
Pool description
destination-port-range
Destination port range
egress-interface
Interface to be used for source interface NAT
egress-network
Network to be used for source interface NAT
icmp-mapping-timeout
ICMP mapping timeout in seconds
provider-org
Public addresses are borrowed from this pool
referenced-outside-nat
Is this pool referenced outside NAT?
routing-instance
Routing instance for this pool
source-port
Source port translation config
tcp-mapping-timeout
TCP mapping timeout in seconds
udp-mapping-timeout
UDP mapping timeout in seconds

Command Mode

Configuration mode

Release Information

Command supported from VOS 22.1.4 and later releases.

set orgs org-services VERSA cgnat pools DIA-Pool-INET-1 source-port

Syntax Description Options

Syntax

set orgs org-services VERSA cgnat pools DIA-Pool-INET-1 source-port (allocation-scheme | block-reserved-ports | exclude-port-range | random-allocation | range)

Description

Source port translation config

Options

allocation-scheme
Port allocation scheme
block-reserved-ports
Block reserved ports
exclude-port-range
Port range to be excluded from the NAT pool
random-allocation
Allocate IP/port randomly
range
Port range to be used for translation

Command Mode

Configuration mode

Release Information

Command supported from VOS 22.1.4 and later releases.

Port allocation scheme

Options

range-based

set orgs org-services VERSA class-of-service

Syntax Description Options

Syntax

set orgs org-services VERSA class-of-service

Description

class-of-service configuration

Options

app-qos-policies
Application QOS policy rules configuration
drop-profiles
class-of-service configuration for drop profiles
forwarding-class-map
Mapping from forwarding-class to traffic-class and queue number
ingress-shaping
Ingress shaping configuration
interfaces
class-of-service configuration for interfaces
networks
class-of-service configuration for networks
qos-policies
QOS policy rules configuration
qos-profiles
QOS profile configuration
rw-rules
rewrite rules config
scheduler-maps
class-of-service configuration for scheduler-maps
schedulers
class-of-service configuration for scheduler

Command Mode

Configuration mode

Release Information

Command supported from VOS 22.1.4 and later releases.

set orgs org-services VERSA class-of-service app-qos-policies

Syntax Description Options

Syntax

set orgs org-services VERSA class-of-service app-qos-policies (Name)

Description

Application QOS policy rules configuration

Options

Name

fc_af

fc_be

fc_ef

fc_nc

Command Mode

Release Information

set orgs org-services VERSA class-of-service rw-rules

Syntax Description Options

Syntax

set orgs org-services VERSA class-of-service rw-rules (copy-from-inner | copy-from-outer | dscp | dscp-ipv6 | ieee-802.1p)

Description

rewrite rules config

Options

copy-from-inner
rewrite string is copied to outer tag from inner
copy-from-outer
rewrite string is copied to inner tag from outer
dscp
rewrite rules for IPv4 DSCP
dscp-ipv6
rewrite rules for IPv6 DSCP
ieee-802.1p
rewrite rules for IEEE 802.1p

Command Mode

Configuration mode

Release Information

Command supported from VOS 22.1.4 and later releases.

Options

settings
Device Identification settings
tag-filter

set orgs org-services VERSA iot-security device-identification settings

Syntax Description Options

Syntax

set orgs org-services VERSA iot-security device-identification settings

Description

Device Identification settings

Options

acceptable-score
Minimum score needed to consider that the device is correctly classified
identification
Identification of Devices
lef-profile
LEF profile for logging
lef-profile-default
Use default logging profile
mdns
MDNS parameters for device identification
message-server
Versa messaging server provider configuration
networks
Do device discovery on specified networks
on-cloud-profile
On cloud profile configuration
on-premise-profile
On-premise profile information
spoof-detection
Enable spoof detection
zones
Match packets from these zones only

set orgs org-services VERSA security captive-portal

Syntax Description Options

Syntax

set orgs org-services VERSA security captive-portal

Description

Captive portal

Options

anchoring
Capitve portal anchoring type
ask-form
Directory location for the form to be displayed for ask action response
auth-failed-page
Directory location of the page to be displayed after authentication request fails
auth-form
Directory location for the form to be displayed for authentication action response
block-page
Directory location of the page to be displayed after blocking the request
cancel-page
Directory location of the page to be displayed after user has canceled the action
cookie-auth-profile
Authentication profile for cookie authentication
custom-redirect-parameters
List of query parameters used while applying custom redirect
expiration-time
Expiration time for the pages (in minutes), pages will be displayed to the user once during this period
inform-form
Directory location for the form to be displayed for inform action response
justify-form
Directory location for the form to be displayed for justify action response
override-form
Directory location for the form to be displayed for override action response
provider-org
Provider org to look for captive portal pages if pages are not configured here
service-endpoints
Captive portal service endpoint configuration
ssl-ca-certificate
Certificate to use for captive portal over SSL
track-by-host
Captive portal tracking by host

Command Mode

Configuration mode

Release Information

Command supported from VOS 22.1.4 and later releases.

set orgs org-services VERSA security captive-portal custom-redirect-parameters

Syntax Description Options

Syntax

set orgs org-services VERSA security captive-portal custom-redirect-parameters

Description

List of query parameters used while applying custom redirect

Options

action
Name of parameters which will specify applied action object name value
mac-address
Name of parameters which will specify MAC address name value
security-policy-rule
Name of parameters which will specify security access policy rule name value
source-ip
Name of parameters which will specify source IP address value
time
Name of parameters which will specify time value
url
Name of parameters which will specify URL value
url-category
Name of parameters which will specify URL-category value
url-reputation
Name of parameters which will specify URL-reputation value
urlf-profile
Name of parameters which will specify url-filtering profile name value
wan-ip
Name of parameters which will specify Versa WAN interface IP address name value

set orgs org-services VERSA layer2-sd-wan

Syntax Description Options

Syntax

set orgs org-services VERSA layer2-sd-wan (application-cache | application-dynamic-detection | forwarding-profiles | policies | sla-profiles | url-category-cache)

Description

SD-WAN policies configuration for Layer 2 traffic

Options

application-cache
Enable/disable application detection using cache at the beginning of flow
application-dynamic-detection
Enable/disable application detection dynamically in the middle of flow
forwarding-profiles
Forwarding profiles to select outgoing link based on SLA requirements
policies
SD-WAN policies configuration for Layer 2 traffic
sla-profiles
Service Level Agreement (SLA) profiles configuration
url-category-cache
Enable/disable URL category detection using cache at the beginning of flow

Command Mode

Configuration mode

Release Information

Command supported from VOS 22.1.4 and later releases.

set orgs org-services VERSA sd-wan

Syntax Description Options

Syntax

set orgs org-services VERSA sd-wan (application-cache | application-dynamic-detection | application-steering | forwarding-profiles | mos-fixed-delay | policies | sla-profiles | url-category-cache)

Description

SD-WAN policies configuration

Options

application-cache
Enable/disable application detection using cache at the beginning of flow
application-dynamic-detection
Enable/disable application detection dynamically in the middle of flow
application-steering
SaaS app autosteering configuration
forwarding-profiles
Forwarding profiles to select outgoing link based on SLA requirements
mos-fixed-delay
Non Network delay in milliseconds for calculatingMOS score
policies
SD-WAN policies configuration
sla-profiles
Service Level Agreement (SLA) profiles configuration
url-category-cache
Enable/disable URL category detection using cache at the beginning of flow

Command Mode

Configuration mode

Release Information

Command supported from VOS 22.1.4 and later releases.

set orgs org-services VERSA url-filtering

Syntax Description Options

Syntax

set orgs org-services VERSA url-filtering (settings | url-tracking | user-defined-url-categories)

Description

URL Filtering Configuration

Options

settings
URL filtering settings
url-tracking
Track URLs based on categories
user-defined-url-categories
Define user defined URL categories

Command Mode

Configuration mode

Release Information

Command supported from VOS 22.1.4 and later releases.

set orgs org-services VERSA url-filtering settings

Syntax Description Options

Syntax

set orgs org-services VERSA url-filtering settings (cloud-lookup | history | logging | match-type | spack | walled-garden)

Description

URL filtering settings

Options

cloud-lookup
URL-filtering cloud lookup configuration
history
Store URLF session history matching the filters; Matches all, If no filters are configured
logging
URL-filtering logging related settings
match-type
Field used to match URL category
spack
Spack configurations
walled-garden
This configuration governs URL categories that need access restrictions prior to authentication

Command Mode

Configuration mode

Release Information

Command supported from VOS 22.1.4 and later releases.

set orgs org-services VERSA url-filtering settings cloud-lookup

Syntax Description Options

Syntax

set orgs org-services VERSA url-filtering settings cloud-lookup (cache-limit | cache-time-to-live | cloud-lookup-profile | mode | query-parameter | state | timeout)

Description

URL-filtering cloud lookup configuration

Options

cache-limit
Maximum number of cloud response cache values to store locally
cache-time-to-live
Cloud response cache TTL value, in seconds
cloud-lookup-profile
Cloud lookup profile to use while connecting to cloud server
mode
Mode to use for cloud lookup request
query-parameter
Enable/Disable query-parameter
state
Enable/Disable URL cloud lookup for all traffic
timeout
Cloud request timeout, in milliseconds

set orgs org-services VERSA url-filtering settings history

Syntax Description Options

Syntax

set orgs org-services VERSA url-filtering settings history (actions | cache-history | categories | profiles | reputations)

Description

Store URLF session history matching the filters; Matches all, If no filters are configured

Options

actions
Save to history, The URLs which are applied by these actions
cache-history
Enable/Disable cache history
categories
Save to history, The URLs matching these categories
profiles
Save to history, The URLs matching these profiles
reputations
Save to history, The URLs having these reputations

set orgs org-services VERSA user-identification settings notification (client-id | client-secret | cloud-profile | state | suppression-interval | timeout)

Description

User threat notification configurations

Options

client-id
Client ID to use while sending request to cloud
client-secret
Client secret to use while sending request to cloud
cloud-profile
SSLCLOUD profile to connect to notification service
state
Enable/Disable threat notifications
suppression-interval
Duplicate notification suppression interval configuration (default 120 seconds)
timeout
Notification timeout configuration (default 30 seconds)

show orgs org |

Syntax Description Options

Syntax

show orgs org |

Description

Configure or display settings for this command path.

Options

append
Append output text to a file
compare
Show differences to running config
context-match
Context match
count
Count the number of lines in the output
csv
Emit table output in CSV format
de-select
Select columns to not include
details
Display details
display
Display options
display-level
Display level
except
Show only text that does not matches a pattern
extended
Show referring elements
find
Search for the first occurrence of a pattern
grep
Case insensitive search
hide
Hide display options
linnum
Enumerate lines in the output
match
Show only text that matches a pattern
match-all
All selected filters must match
match-any
At least one filter must match
more
Paginate output
nomore
Suppress pagination
save
Save output text to a file
select
Select additional columns
sort-by
Select sorting indices
tab
Enforce table output
until
Display until the first occurrence of a pattern

vfp

Command Mode

Operational mode

Release Information

Command supported from VOS 22.1.4 and later releases.

show bgp

Syntax Description Options

Syntax

show bgp (flap-stats | graceful-restart | group | mpls-vpn-core-info | neighbor | summary)

Description

Display BGP information

Options

flap-stats
Display the details of damped routes
graceful-restart
Display BGP graceful-restart information
group
Display the BGP group database
mpls-vpn-core-info
Displays mpls core bgp information
neighbor
Display BGP neighbor information
summary
Display a summary of BGP neighbor status

show pim

Syntax Description Options

Syntax

show pim

Description

Display PIM information

Options

anycast-rps
Display PIM Anycast RP information
crp-mappings
Display Candidate RP mappings
elected-bsr
Display elected BSR information
interface
Show PIM interface state
join
Show PIM join/prune state
neighbor
Display PIM neighbor information
rps
Display PIM RP information
statistics
Display PIM statistics

show igmp

Syntax Description Options

Syntax

show igmp (group | group-summary | interface | org | ROUTING | NAME | TEST-100)

Description

Displays Tenant bgp information

Options

group
Display information about membership for all IGMP groups
group-summary
Display summary of IGMP groups per Tenant
interface
Display information about IGMP enabled interfaces
org

router
Router link-state advertisements
routing-instance

summary

31 commands

show interfaces

Syntax Description Options

Syntax

show interfaces

Description

Interfaces configuration

Options

brief
Brief Information of Interfaces
cable-diagnostics
Cable diagnostics port enet-0/x module info
detail
Status of active interfaces
dynamic-tunnels
Information about dynamic tunnels
info
Interfaces Information for Dashboard
ip
Interface IP configuration
org
Display per tenant information
p2mp-gre-stats
Interface statistics for p2mp-gre tunnels
poe
Display Power over Ethernet information
port
All port statistics
sfpinfo
SFP port vni-0/x module info
statistics
Interface statistics
wwan
wwan modem

Command Mode

Operational mode

Release Information

Command supported from VOS 22.1.4 and later releases.

show log audit

Syntax Description Options

Syntax

show log audit (end-time | raw | reverse | start-time | Skipping)

Description

Configure or display settings for this command path.

Options

end-time
Stop processing logs after this timestamp (ISO date format)
raw
Show raw file (log which does not follow ISO date format)
reverse
Reverse chronological order
start-time
Start processing logs from this timestamp (ISO date format)
Skipping

Command Mode

Operational mode

Release Information

Command supported from VOS 22.1.4 and later releases.

show commit

Syntax Description Options

Syntax

show commit (changes | list | changes | list)

Description

Configure or display settings for this command path.

Options

changes
Changes for a given rollback id
list
Show commit history
changes
Changes for a given rollback id
list
Show commit history

Operational mode

Release Information

Command supported from VOS 22.1.4 and later releases.

show vsync

Syntax Description Options

Syntax

show vsync (ha | IP | TYPE | Preferred | Current | Standby | RECHECK | TIMEOUT)

Description

Vsync configuration

Options

ha
Vsync HA configuration
IP
TYPE
Preferred
-
Current
-
Standby
-
RECHECK
TIMEOUT

Operational mode

Release Information

Command supported from VOS 22.1.4 and later releases.

show info-validation

Syntax Description Options

Syntax

show info-validation (conf-validation | summary)

Options

conf-validation
Information validation configuration
summary

Command Mode

Operational mode

Release Information

Command supported from VOS 22.1.4 and later releases.

show configuration interfaces

Syntax Description Options

Syntax

show configuration interfaces

Description

Versa xDSL configuration

Options

ae0
ptvi421
ptvi422
tvi-0/22
VXLAN Tunnel Interface for TEST-100 Control VR
tvi-0/23
ESP Tunnel Interface for TEST-100 Control VR
tvi-2/2
WAN side Split Tunnel interface between INET-1 and TEST-100-Enterprise
tvi-2/3
LAN side Split Tunnel interface between INET-1 and TEST-100-Enterprise
tvi-2/4
WAN side Split Tunnel interface between INET-2 and TEST-100-Enterprise
tvi-2/5
LAN side Split Tunnel interface between INET-2 and TEST-100-Enterprise
vni-0/0
vni-0/1
vni-0/2
vni-0/3
vni-0/4
Possible
aggregated-ethernet-options
Specify options for this Aggregated Ethernet link
bandwidth
Interface bandwidth in kbps
description
Description for the interface
dhcp-trusted
DHCP trusted port.
dynamic-interface
Dynamic port profile will be applied
enable
Enable/disable this interface
ether-options
Specify Ethernet options
ethernet-segment
EVPN multihoming configuration for Ethernet segment
hold-time
Hold the link state in down state for configured interval before declaring it as operational when transitioning from down to up state.
mirror-interface
Used for traffic mirroring
mode
Interface mode
mtu
MTU of the interface
native-vlan-id
Specifies the Native VLAN ID for the interface
next-routing-instance-nexthop
Use route table of other end of the paired TVI for route lookup.
nsh-capable
Enable NSH header encapsulation
outer-tpid
Outer VLAN tag protocol id supported on the port
paired-interface
Other interface for local tunnel
parent-interface
Parent tvi interface of type p2mp
poe
Power over Ethernet
pppoe-base-interface
Used as PPPoE base interface
promiscuous
Enable or disable promiscuous mode on this interface
remote-address
IP address of tunnel destination endpoint
service-function-nsh
Act as a IETF sfc service function with no service function forwarder configuration
sfp-options
Specify SFP options
standby-options
Standby configuration
transport-type
Choice of transport encapsulation
type
Tunnel type
unit
Configure default logical interface under this interface
virtual-wire
Member of virtual wire pair

promiscuous

Command Mode

Operational mode

Release Information

Command supported from VOS 22.1.4 and later releases.

show configuration routing-instances

Syntax Description Options

Syntax

show configuration routing-instances

Description

Routing instance configuration

Options

GUEST
Possible
instance-type
Type of routing instance;;Default virtual-router
scalable-group-tag
Scalable group tag configuration
bridge-options
Bridge options
policy-options
Routing policy option configuration
bridge-domains
bridge domain global configuration
usage-type
Usage type of routing instance
global-vrf-id
Global vrf id of routing instance.
traffic-director-global-vrf-id
Second Global vrf id of routing instance.
networks
List of network names
mpls-vpn-core
This routing instance can be used as mpls-vpn-core-instance for other VRFs
mpls-vpn-core-instance
Name of MPLS transport routing instance for this VRF; specify 'global' to use the default instance rather than a specific named routing-instance as the core instance
vrf-evpn-core-instance
Name of the EVPN core routing instance responsible for BGP signaling of this VRF; specify 'global' to use the default instance rather than a specific named routing-instance as the core instance
reverse-traffic-instance
Name of routing instance reverse flow creation in the case of intelligent-traffic-director; specify 'global' to use the default instance
evpn-core
This routing instance can be used as core-instance for virtual-switch routing-instances with EVPN
cloud-export-instance
This routing instance can be used for Cloud File export
description
Text description of routing instance
interfaces
List of Interface names
route-distinguisher
Route distinguisher for this instance.
vrf-import-target
Target community to use when filtering L3VPN routes on import.
vrf-export-target
Target community to use when exporting L3VPN routes.
vrf-both-target
Target community to use when exporting L3VPN routes and when filtering them on import.
vni
vxlan VNI to be signaled through EVPN for this IP VRF
routing-options
Protocol-independent routing option configuration
protocols
Routing protocol configuration
GUEST
INET-1-Transport-VR
instance-type
policy-options

Release Information

Command supported from VOS 22.1.4 and later releases.

show configuration system

Syntax Description Options

Syntax

show configuration system

Description

System configuration and statistics

Options

arp
Session timeout configuration
banner-text
CLI/SSH/MOTD banners
bw-measurement
system level bandwidth measurement commands
confd-parameters
console
Console connection config parameters
core-profile
dataplane-latency-measurement

device
dhcp-parameters
System level DHCP parameters config
dns
Domain name system
external-aaa
External Authentication/Authorization/Accounting
identification
System Identification
log-compress
Log compression/rotation parameters
login
Configure login monitoring parameters
maintenance-mode
Maintenance mode
parameters
password-policy
Configure login monitoring parameters
platform
Platform specific configurations
sd-wan
Global parameters for SDWAN appliance
security
service-options
Service options configuration (vsboot parameters)
service-scaling
Service scaling configuration
services
Enable/Disable system/Linux services via datapath
session
Session timeout configuration
smart-aggregator
ssh
Configure SSH parameters
subjugate
Subjugation parameters (master Versa-Director IP)
syslog
Syslog Server
time-zone
Set system time-zone
traffic-director
configure traffic-director related
upgrade-options
Upgrade options
user-lockout
Configure user lockout(faillock)
users
Add users to the system

wwan

Release Information

Command supported from VOS 22.1.4 and later releases.

show configuration security

Syntax Description Options

Syntax

show configuration security (osspack | secure-download | security-file-collection | security-package | security-file-collection | location | checksum)

Description

Security configuration

Options

osspack
OS Security package
secure-download
Enable secure download (only HTTPS)
security-file-collection
Security tar file information
security-package
Security package configuration
security-file-collection
location
checksum

Command Mode

Operational mode

Release Information

Command supported from VOS 22.1.4 and later releases.

show configuration protocols lldp

Syntax Description Options

Syntax

show configuration protocols lldp (advertisement-interval | enable | hold-multiplier | lldp-interface | No)

Description

LLDP configuration

Options

advertisement-interval
Time interval for sending hello BPDUs(Default 30 seconds)
enable
Enable LLDP on all interfaces (Default disabled)
hold-multiplier
Sets value for the LLDP hold multiplier(Default 4 seconds)
lldp-interface
Interface options for LLDP
No

Release Information

Command supported from VOS 22.1.4 and later releases.

show configuration protocols vrrp

Syntax Description Options

Syntax

show configuration protocols vrrp (alarm-logs-soak-time | mac-address-mode | version | vrrp-v3 | No)

Description

VRRP global configuration

Options

alarm-logs-soak-time
Soak period (in seconds) before alarm logs are reported
mac-address-mode
Use physical interface MAC address or virtual MAC address for virtual IP(s)
version
Select VRRP version (default: v3)
vrrp-v3
VRRP version 3 parameters
No

show configuration security security-package

Syntax Description Options

show configuration security security-package

Description

Security package configuration

Options

auto-update
automatic download and installation of security packages.
download-timeout
Security package download timeout
download-type
Security package download type
flavor
Security-package flavor
realtime-auto-update
automatic download and installation of security real time update.
routing-instance
Routing instance to be used for spack network operation
routing-instances

sanity-validation

url

No

Command Mode

Release Information

Command supported from VOS 22.1.4 and later releases.

request system

Syntax Description Options

Syntax

request system

Description

System configuration and statistics

Options

bw-measurement
system level bandwidth measurement commands
configuration
Configuration actions for the appliance
cpu-watchdog
Command to enable the cpu hardware watchdog monitor
create-snapshot
Create a snapshot (known-good) state
db-backup
Take periodic backup of config and operational data
dynamic-provision
System-level dynamic-provisioning commands
fips-mode
firmware
Firmware management for WiFi and T1/E1 cards on CSG devices
gps
Show device location
hypervisor

isolate-cpu
Isolate CPUs from the kernel scheduler
isolcpu
Isolate CPUs from the kernel scheduler (DEPRECATED)
kernel-crashdump
Command to enable the kernel crash dump
load-default
Reset the appliance to default device configuration
login
Configure login monitoring parameters
logout
User operations
package
Package management
platform-watchdog
Command to enable the platform hardware watchdog monitor
reboot
Reboot the appliance
reset
Reset the appliance to default device configuration
restart
Restart Versa applications
rollback
Rollback to a given snapshot (known-good state)
save-custom-default
Save the current configuration as default device configuration

scaleout

secure-mode

Stop Versa applications

unlock-user
Unlock a locked user
user-lockout

request system package

Syntax Description Options

Syntax

request system package (check-integrity | delete | downgrade | fetch | list | upgrade)

Description

Package management

Options

check-integrity
Validate checksum of Versa FlexVNF bin
delete
Delete a package (from disk)
downgrade
Downgrade Versa appliance to the given package (deletes entire running config)
fetch
Fetch a package (from remote host)
list
List available packages (on disk)
upgrade
Upgrade Versa appliance to the given package

password

remotefile

server
Hostname or IP address of remote server
username
User name for scp
X

Release Information

Command supported from VOS 22.1.4 and later releases.

request clear statistics dhcp-snooping

Syntax Description Options

Syntax

request clear statistics dhcp-snooping (all | org)

Description

Clear DHCP snooping statistics

Options

all
Clear DHCP snooping statistics of all tenants
org
Clear DHCP snooping statistics of a tenant

Operational mode

Release Information

Command supported from VOS 22.1.4 and later releases.

Destination port or port range (e.g.
destination-prefix
Destination IP prefix
filter-name
Session filter name
ip-version
IP version/family (default IPv4)
modules
Module specific logging
protocol
Protocol value
routing-instance
Routing instance name
source-port
Source port or port range (e.g.
source-prefix
Source IP prefix
Value

Accepted Values
